reviews.review_description:
When I first started out I didn't think much of this . But as soon as I started scratching the surface it opens up big time.
It's giving me serious empyrion galactic survival vibes.  Im way int this now and highly recommend this. It's a really well balanced survival experience.

developer_response.body: Happy to hear you're enjoying this, CryptoSage! Thank you for playing and taking the time to leave us a review.

reviews.review_title: Great game but not fully cooked
reviews.review_description: Spent a long time on this game and finally completed it. The environment is really cool. The enemy drones were all the same silly looking design. I played on medium difficulty and i literally never had to use a health pack because everything does barely any damage and your health automatically regenerates so fast. I love the crafting and the base building. It actually works really well when you get used to it. It was cool I could move a machine or box without removing items. It was pretty cool growing my own food. I eventually notice certain crafting materials weren’t used, like why is this in the game. Once I picked stuff up I wasn’t sure how to delete it so extra stuff like message logs would just take up much needed slots. About 2/3 of the way, after you build a rover, there is literally nothing else to build. It lets you unlock these rare crafting materials though that you can sale and nothing else. It was kind of confusing. There was no way to get enough points for you to max out your suit abilities until you unlock the towers, and then the game is pretty much over. I was pretty disappointed with the ending. I was hoping i would be able to fix, repair, find pieces to a shuttle but you literally just pull a lever. It would be great to be able to prepare for your voyage home, make sure you have enough fuel, food, and water for the voyage. Hopefully they add more in and I might come back for a hard playthrough
Such a great immersive escape & adventure. Figuring out how to build your habitat & machines while exploring the planet while the sun & stars pass by overhead is a trip. Really digging this.

I loved playing this game. I thought the gameplay itself was great and worked really well. This game desperately needs a version 2, or preferably another 10 zones to the south as an add on, so you can keep your existing base and carry on exploring. Either way it was sad when I came to the end. Well done guys.

developer_response.body: Congratulations, you've survived Mars! Thank you for playing and for leaving a review, Dom68 🫡

reviews.review_description: I love survival games & space so this game was a dream come true lol. The graphics look so good and the rocket blaster is sooo fun to use. There is plenty of activities between building & upgrading your habitat and exploring new areas. I will say that it was maybe too easy to accomplish the end goal. Id play any additional levels or content in a heartbeat. Thanks to the developers for such a fun & unique experience.

I am only a couple hours in and finally understand how things work and it's starting to click with me.  Very addictive gameplay loop.  Good graphics and audio.  Mechanics work well once you figure them out although I wish it had more physical interactions rather than point and click.  Overall a solid game👍

developer_response.body: Hey APEBoys, thanks for leaving us a positive review! Your feedback regarding the controls is noted, and it's good to know that the core gameplay loop has been an enjoyable experience. Remember to keep an eye on your oxygen levels and we'll see you on Mars. Good luck, Pioneer!

reviews.review_description: Best game on vr….so much to do and not too hard either.

developer_response.body: That is high praise, thank you so much!

reviews.review_description:
This is a fun VR basebuilder, but there is a VERY common bug that will prevent you from being able to finish the game. Around 1/3 of the way through the game, you will reach a mission that tells you to touch the object to interact with it and you likely won't be able to. This is a bug and your entire game is broken. 

It seems the developers rushed this game to full release so they could abandon it. I highly doubt this bug will ever get fixed, nor this game will ever see any more updates. Absolutely cannot recommend.

This has been my absolute favorite game on the Quest. Lots to figure out, graphics are great, loved driving the hover bike and rover. Challenging, but I wasn't afraid something was going to pop out and kill me all the time. I just finished the game today and will undoubtedly start a new game in the future.  I would love to see more options available for the inside of the habitats. More furniture to build: chairs, kitchen, windows, etc. Also the loot gets a bit boring, and could be much more interesting.  Please keep updating this game developers, it was really fun!

I really love this game, the graphics and atmosphere are top quality. The grind/upgrade system is really fun as you can jetpack and use the rover/bike to collect materials. It really is a joy to play.
Beautiful and amazingly immersive, this game lets you experience what it would feel like to be alone on Mars with just your natural human drive to survive. I love it!

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing, Vamperion750, and thank you for taking the time to write us a review!

reviews.review_description: Its fun, but now i'm getting the point, Developers is Flat2VR studios when will Roboquest VR come out, please i need to know early 2026 isn't all i need to hear, please i beg of you tell me when, i want to play it...

I got this game around Christmas and I’ve been into it since. It is great, very interactive, lots of things to figure out. There was a serious bug that kept me from solving a number of puzzles but the developers did a patch this week that solved the issue. Really enjoying this adventure, one of the best I’ve played!
I desperately hope that they work on a multiplayer option

reviews.review_title: Best quest game ive played But...
reviews.review_description: I love this game I thought about it at work before bed I couldn't wait to continue playing it but there's a bug at the end of the game we're I can't interact with the puzzles I need to to finish the game it seems like a common problem.

The game grabbed my attention right away. The concept is really interesting. Building, managing resources, and trying to survive on Mars feels immersive and thoughtful. There’s a strong sense of progression as you figure things out and slowly expand your base.

I especially liked the early game. Learning the systems, exploring the environment, and getting everything up and running felt rewarding and strategic without being overwhelming but then…it became over-repetitive. 

That said, it didn’t completely hook me the whole way through. At times the pacing can feel a little slow or repetitive like I said which kept it from being a full 5-star experience for me.

Overall though, it’s still a solid and unique survival/management game, especially if you enjoy space or base-building sims. Definitely worth trying.

reviews.review_title: Bug in Roswell and Widowspan fixed
reviews.review_description: I have thoroughly enjoyed playing this game.  The Quest 3 controller use was a bit confusing to start with and the basics of how to eat and get oxygen to stay alive at the beginning was not so clear, but with perseverance it turned out to be a good enjoyable game that i have spent many hours in.  My only issue was in the final worlds of Widowspan and Roswell cross.  There was a bug in the quest game whereby neither the discs or cubes will activate.  The devs have now fixed this and I am almost at the end of a very enjoyable game. I highly recommend this game

I love the game. It's peaceful, strategic, immersive, etc., etc. 

However, the bug where I cannot move the stones in the Charleston base is very annoying! Please correct this. 

Update! Bug is history, I can play again 🥳

Love the game!

So many absorbing hours exploring Mars! The glitches have been fixed and the game is really satisfying to play. I hope there will be a discord soon since it’s a bit difficult to find answers to my questions anywhere… but otherwise couldn’t be happier.

I could not stop playing! So many exciting places to go and you can personalize your home and build all kinds of things! Very realistic with oxygen limits and nutrition levels. A warning, however, to any of you who want to create a separate game on the same v.r set, it is way too easy to overwrite all of your hard work. It doesn’t warn you that you’re overwriting a saved game. I learned that the hard way and overwrote my dads game and couldn’t fix it. A warning would be nice.
Loved every minute until a game-breaking bug in widowspan.  Can’t activate disks.  Tried loading an old save to no avail.  Just hoping the devs fix it soon.

reviews.review_title: Amazing, but has some rough edges
reviews.review_description:
First of all, this is a great game, easily 5 stars. Closest to Subnautica i have seen so far. I have a lot of questions/recommendations though.

I played the campaign in normal mode. 

In no particular order:

1. Radioactive environments don't harm me. I have never needed to buy the radiation, cold, heat protection.
2. Storms dont do damage, only the lightning strikes in an ion storm
3. I feel the oxygen canisters give far too much oxygen. I never felt pressured for oxygen or the need to strategize around it. In fact, food was more of an issue because i could only eat in pressured environments (which is realistic and i loved it).
4. I think there should be more story, i played the campaign,.got the achievements and i still have no clue what was going on.
5. I don't fully understand how i earned new blueprints to research. They just appeared on my terminal every so often. 
6. I am not sure if 'blueprint' is the correct term for preprinted modules i can deploy.
7. I am no fan of the way the map is separated in 10 small areas. This is probably a technical choice? I feel this could've been done differently, maybe by hiding the loading screen in a travel animation or something. Anything to make the world feel more open.
8. I really, really missed windows in my base. The viewpod isn't it.
9. It seems i can only build bases in the landing area? I feel that ahould be free to build anywhere. That would give more freedom to strategize.
10. The ENVAS never did anything for me. I couldn't figure out how it worked.
11. I got the 10 tower achievement on the 9th tower :-p
12. Solar and wind should have some difference.. solar should maybe only work during the day and not during storms.. etc.. the transformator could function as a battery.. this would me more realistic and interesting.

I'm sure there is more, but this is wat i could come up with.

Nevertheless this is an amazing experience and a must play for survival/explorer fans. It really conveys the atmosphere of being alone on mars, it is gorgeous and haunting at times.

Thank you.

My experience with surviving mars pioneer was that the game is still in early stages with a lot of room for improvement but for being in the early stages it is a pretty good. I will defidently be playing this more.
This is a great resource gathering, crafting/base building survival game, with enjoyable exploration via foot, vehicles, and jetpack!

You can’t lose with this one. Let’s be clear; the graphics are stunning, the gameplay is smooth, the interface is intuitive, the story is compelling, and the tasks are achievable. I was waiting for this game to come out and the wait was worth it. 

I have adult ADHD. Any and all concentration problems I may have are completely ignored and forgotten during my tenure as an astronaut and adventurer on Mars. Finally, I have yet to experience a single bug or glitch. Now, back to Mars.

This is the most addictive VR game I have ever played. There is a slight learning curve and once you figure it out you won't be able to stop playing.

I hope the devs create an upate soon. I encountered a couple bugs. Nothing that ruined gameplay but a game this good should be perfected.

This is a great survival, exploration game with some puzzles and story. It has hours of game play. Performance on my 3s was solid and smooth with only a few very minor hangs late game at the base when it got larger and in the rover. I hope the devs  continue to work on and expand the game, it has a good solid base with lots of potential to build on.

Mars has been my favorite planet since I was a kid. To be able to experience it this way is breathtaking. I'm early on in the game, but so far, I like everything I've seen. We'll see how it goes, but this game alone makes me glad I purchased my Meta 3.

reviews.review_title: This is what VR is all about.
reviews.review_description:
Purely from an atmospheric and visual standpoint (paired with Quest Games Optimizer)it looks great and worth the entry price alone. But most importantly it embodies what VR is all about: making you feel like you have been transported to another world.

Standing on Mars looking up at the hazy sky, not a soul nearby, with gentle desert winds blowing through your ears is transformative.

Love the game until its too dark to travel around when your outside! So I sit their and wait for it to become light enough to see! how often is that you ask? I didn't time it but it seems like around every 10 minutes or so!
Please why make it so that a modern space vessal not have a f#*king set of headlights on the vehicle. Or on the space suit or a flashlight to use? Hello out there! Fix it pretty please! I love you long time!

Glowing positive thoughts: I was surprisingly pleased with this game. It took me a minute to figure out what I was doing, but then I started making progress and was invested. I started to enjoy collecting and growing my base. I would keep playing for one more thing to build! I finally said I'd go to bed and was looking forward to continuing, but sadly I didn't manually save and the auto save didn't happen. Even still, now I know what to do and am looking forward to building it back up!

Big shoutout to the level designer for the smart verticality and the sky artist for that beautiful, very-Mars sky! The game loop is wonderfully addictive and well-balanced – progression pulls you, survival keeps you on your toes. It truly nails the feel of surviving Mars solo.

Purchased on a whim, best game for me so far on Quest 3. Interesting exploring Mars and the 10 different areas to complete the game. So far have spent almost 40 hours and have been to 4 locations.  Not only survive but need to earn money for the purchase of advancement. Fulfill the contracts given to you to proceed. Hint -  agree to all contracts if not they will disappear, so agree to all, even if they make no sense or unable to fill right away.  Description says "in app purchases", but have not experience that myself. Bought the game on sale and have not spent any more cash since. Also no shooting, which I  like, but if you want some heart raising scares, just run out of oxygen or unable to consume food on the way back to base. Save game often during play, just in case. There is auto-save but it's best to do the save yourself. I personally recommend this game! Woo Hoo !! Time to head back to base.

I've had this game since early access, and even in early access this game felt so well polished, despite a few minor bugs. It has only gotten better since then. There's so much good here to appreciate,  don't even know where to begin!

TL:DR- If the concept of the game looks interesting to you, it's as awesome as it looks!

They really tightened down the interaction handling in menus and with physical objects, and the feedback from the controllers. There are a lot of little touches with the controllers giving you just a little pulse in combination with an onscreen highlight that is just super intuitive. Most of the time you don't even think about it.

The game runs super smooth all the time, I don't think I've ever experienced any slowdowns, it feels like you're just inside the game.

The progression level is great. You start small, learn the basics of the game one step at a time so you don't get overwhelmed.  You can't travel far in the beginning due to oxygen limits and your food sources don't get you far. Slowly you expand, go further and further.

The game really creates an environment where you need to think ahead and figure out what resources you'll need to bring with you to go explore and be able to stay out there without having to run back to your base.

The base building and setting up power lines is great. I appreciate that they didn't take a SimCity approach in making you destroy structures if you don't like how they're placed. You're free to either move them directly or pull them back to inventory and place them out again.

I think I saw some comments about the controls being confusing on the rover and hoverboard, I definitely disagree. They are virtual handles that have a tank control, but it's smooth. I do also like how the hoverbike is still relevant even after unlocking the rover. You'd think the rover would replace it, but the hoverbike is great for nice fast exploring.

The visuals are absolutely stunning! The envir. I ran out of room. Just get it!

I loved this game, I rarely incountered any bugs, only crashed a few times, nothing major. Base building is fun and easy to understand. Great game if you like games like No Man's sky, Astroneer, or Subnautica. Exploration is fun and can be creepy your first time around, as some interiors have no power. Story is great and I wish it had a part two so we can learn more about the many secrets of the game's world. Overall 10/10.

Update: It now has Quest Achievements and is one of my top 5 games of 2025! 

It's a stunning trip to Mars! Just needs Quest Achievements!

Right off the bat, love the visuals. It would be nice if the flashlight feature could be used by the player and possibly brighter in the caves. 
The only bugs I have encountered are
1. Every now and then random items from my stored inventory can be found hovering around in random places. I'm riding the hoverbike and I see some aluminum just hanging in the sky.
2. Occasionally I get stuck under the hoverbike if I accidentally ride into one of the canyons. So, save frequently!
Other than that, great game! It would be cool if I could play with a friend, not a bunch...just one. 
I hope the upcoming material is as good as it has been. 
Off to a fantastic start!

This game is a great concept and really fun at times, but the vast amount of bugs make it absolutely unplayable at times.  I cannot count the amount of times I have entered a building only to find that i am either stuck 'inbetween worlds' where i am not really in the map or outside of the map but stuck between.  Or the amount of times I have fallen through the map after simply exiting the bike.  Let's talk about the monitors on the machines... OMG.  When the game is bugging out, you have to do this silly little dance of moving back and forth and side to side to somehow find the sweetspot that allows the monitors to go from blacked out to displaying its information properly.  I know this is early release, but my god man these are very basic aspects to the game... release a patch please.  My advice:  SAVE OFTEN.  Save before you exit the bike, save before you enter a building.  I am done w early release games after this.

I really feel like I’m living and working on Mars. I haven’t played for very many hours yet, but I’m really enjoying it. The scenery is gorgeous. All of the controls and functions are working excellent.￼ the crafting is so much fun. Thank you developers!

I can't wait til I get the next materials and the next pieces in order to upgrade and expand my Mars base. I already made walls, furniture, lots of food and enough oxygen capsules to take on my next exploring journey. 
In future updates, I wish for a mirror I could see myself and my suit upgrades.
And if you haven't yet (I havent discovered yet) we need an arcade machine in our base in order to spend play tim while we wait for the heavy weather storms to pass by, maybe the arcade game could play in the wall display, that would be even more awesome.
Good job, developers, awesome awesome game!
I love this game so much but I stopped playing it because there’s no way to figure out the name of the towers and what regions they are in. It just turned into a huge waste of time, what a shame. ￼

I love these kinds of games where you spend hours in VR, collecting, crafting, exploring, building, and surviving. Whether it's Green Hell, Minecraft, QuestCraft, or Medieval Dynasty, these games work so well in VR! Surviving Mars is at the top of its games when it comes to Atmosphere, Sound, and immersion. You feel like a stranded Matt Damon, alone on the Red Planet.

The Sound is really well done, whether it's the Music, voice acting, or wind noises, everything feels just right.
And the haptic's are awesome to.

However, I think the graphics on the Quest 3 could be a bit better. I'd like to see a few small effects like dust devils passing by, or the occasional shooting star in the sky, but those are minor things. What I really like is the interaction. You can grab things you've crafted and put them in your backpack. You have to pull levers, steer vehicles, grab your backpack and the map. I'd like to see the same for the tools (scanner, contractor, extractor).

For me this is one of the best game releases in 2025. And I would really like to see, that development doesn't end with the release of Surviving Mars 1.0 and  we get even more Content for this game, whether DLCs or an Updates.

This is probably the best game I have played on VR.   You collect resources,   build your entire habitat from the ground up,  grow your food, launch rockets to get paid to buy more blueprints for items,  explore other lands to scavenge for more resources.. robots are shooting at you there, so be careful 😄 🤣   and you Jam towers all over.  Watch out for the storms and ion storm with lightning 🌩 bolts!  That scared me so bad that I jumped when it struck next to me.  Lol 

Awesome job, Devs!!  Love it 😀
Also, I love your other game, Genotype!   That's how I knew I would love this one.
Needs some time to get used to the controls, but I was hooked pretty much from the beginning. Great graphics and fun gameplay. Highly recommended.

Three and a half hours in and the feeling of abject isolation that this game creates is testament to the developers, you really feel as if you're alone on Mars and every decision counts, the mechanics are pretty solid (occasional  glitches are few and far between, absolutely nothing game-breaking). The game doesn't hold your hand either, as I struggled to work out the Aluminium problem, before becoming elated when I cracked it, which is the kind of feeling you want from a game of this type. I'm not normally the type that plays these 'Survival' style games, but I have to say this is instantly into my top three VR games of the year so far. Congratulations to all involved.

I first bought the game awhile ago and wasn't able to play it after a certain point due to a bug.  So I refunded it.  But I was able to reach out to the devs and they fixed the issue. I repurchase the game and everything works great. It's s very fun game and addicting. I like the ability to make the game easier or harder. If you want to just explore and not worry about oxygen and nutrition, you can turn it off. If you want to make it really difficult. You can as well. Games definitely worth the price. You can spend a ton of time playing this game. It has so much content to keep you coming back for more. If you ever come across a bug, the devs will reach out quickly to resolve the issue.

developer_response.body: Hi pwolfe! We sincerely appreciate having the opportunity to chat with you about the issues you were having, and your willingness to give SMP another try. We hope you have fun!

reviews.review_description: It’s a cool game, nice story and gameplay. It’s nice, but, do you know how when you get a new game and you want to point around and do a thumbs up, in game? (Like if you do that too) It doesn’t happen, you can only have a hand closed or opened, that’s it. Controls are very tight. It’s not a great opening experience until you just accept it that. But either that that, it’s fun

I'm stuck between buildings and I don't know what to do. It's a major bug because it keeps happening every time. Even when I'm restarting my game. I dont want to lose my progress. Please add stuck button on start menu.

reviews.review_title: Its a great game but still has bugs
reviews.review_description: Great game, too bad I had 2 times where i got stuck in a random place during a mission and then suddenly I appeared in my home with no items in inventory (items important to finish the quest). If your items disappear because of a crash they will not appear again neither at the location where you picked them initially neither in your inventory. I had to load again the game from my previous saves to get the key back to finish the mission and lost hours of gameplay. Please make it that if you get stuck and the game replaces you back in base, to put the items back in their place in the mission.
I've spent 10+ hours so far. Excellent controls and decent visuals. I'm a huge fan of this style of gameplay and look forward to updates.

I love this game, I've played it so many times and built habitats so many different ways that when the new update came out I was extremely excited. New caves, new machinery. I hope they come out with another survival game like surviving saturn just like this game cuz its soooo addicting. Please make another game like this!!!!!!
Ever play that space version of roller coaster tycoon? Like an amazing first person version of that! I have only played for like 2 hours or so and just love it! No notes so far
Love the visuals and the interactive story is so fun, it is not that hard it is a medium difficulty, for all you younger kids. It is one of the best games I own, it is definitely worth it.

I got this when it came out as early access. I'm usually skeptical, but even in early access there was tons of stuff to do. Then I saw the complete version was out and they had added the ending. I decided to replay from scratch and they added even more content aside from the ending. It's a super fun game, tons of content & alot of hours of play. You definitely won't feel ripped off. I've read other reviews where people talk about bugs, but I have NEVER had any problems even in early access. Still no problems. Everything has always worked smoothly. I think some people do stupid things like trying to go places they aren't supposed to or maybe their headsets are just crappy.  I really enjoyed it. Thank you to the creaters. There have been alot of disappointing games coming out this year. This is one I didn't want to put down. I'll definitely replay it again in the future.

This game was so fun to play. I probably played it nonstop for a couple weeks or so. Although there isn't much for background music, so I usually play music from Wall-E, Interstellar, and The Martian on Spotify while I play, but it is so fun, and really feels as if you're on Mars. The only thing other than music is that I've never been able to find the last drone from the final zone to finish the game. Other then that, this game is already a favorite of mine. Absolutely worth it!
Dissatisfied with the fact that this was and early access game, and the latest update was a 10 minute ending.  Is this all for this game or will there be more maps and updates?
Breathtaking. Thank you! 😃

This game reminds me of little cities mixed with elements of into black and a little of a mobile game with the resource gathering.  So far I've been sucked it. The visuals look fine they ain't red matter levels but are fine. The skybox and the district objects add to the environment. The skybox is unique and not static as other sky boxes.  I think the game needs a tutorial that's a little more and the mini map should have a label system or way point unless your going to add a or have a good photographic memory of what all the  icons mean. I can say it's close to into the radius as I'm still in the beginning area. Unlocking my base. In about an end to science and city building we'll find a game interesting.

the polish on the full release is good i'll prob start a new game since it took a long break to get back familiar with all the new things.

reviews.review_title: PLEASE PROVIDE OPTION TO DISABLE HUD!!!!
reviews.review_description: This is just first impressions as i have only just started playing the game. Visuals are amazing for stand alone but periphery view is entirely blocked by a very noticeable and conspicuous helmet. PLEASE DEVS, GIVEV US AN OPTION TO DISABLE THIS!!
I really love space and sandbox and then I find this but it has an issue (on the title)

Have been here since early access and I love how the game has evolved. My only two complaints is that the upgrades for Radiation, Heat, and Cold protection don’t really do anything and NGL I wish there was a way we could fight back against the drones, like at least something to stun them or something especially the new ones in camp 63’s interior

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing Jordan! The radiation/heat/cold upgrades not working are a known bug we're trying to get to the bottom of, it would be super helpful if you used the in-game report button to send us some info. You can use the mining laser to damage drones. Hope this helps!

reviews.review_title: Do I have the full game now ?
reviews.review_description:
Hey developers ,

Dim question ( sorry ) but as an early access adopter do I now have full access to the full game ? No extra charges ??

Cheers guys .





Old Final review / summary 😊

It’s Early Access so I guess we wait for the fully , completed , version to launch once all of the issues etc. are dealt with ?

With Oxygen and Nutrition set to zero I have accomplished quite a lot without the fear of death due to running around madly for ice. A very good thing indeed!

Building is straight forward enough once you have gathered enough resources however have noticed that multiple scenarios require multiple amounts of the same resource which can be a tricky planning exercise to get right.  I guess this is the intention? Also do you store resources in a box or your inventory . You have to check this often so you can make something else?

I think the way you create a production line method of making in your habitat makes sense but can be quite complex to mitigate and execute efficiently.

Is there a console for breaking unwanted items down and reclaiming the core materials back? Would help.

Furniture and other items to complement your habitat factory are limited so far and would need to be quite expansive in the final version to allow creativity and interior design to feature as much as resource gathering. A bigger manufacturing facility should surely feature in the final version ?

Walking or driving the bike is good , the hover bike is excellent , and you explore with relative ease and soak up the Martian vibe. Excellent so far.
Hopefully a larger Mars rover with truck sized storage will also arrive , as leaving items because your inventory is full is a current pain.

Some graphic details are a bit simple , so they must be an early iteration of what’s to came ? Other details and animations are superb , so just need to wait and see.

So in final summary ,

It’s great and clearly offers scope for so much more to come.
Survival on Mars will be a hellish ordeal and will actually result in many premature deaths but a VR game should offer some actual hope and reward without the fear of failure ( completely ).

Looking forward to seeing how this develops.

Don’t forget us early explorers still out there 👍

Good luck guys ( developers ).

developer_response.body: Hi Bert! Yes! You already own the full release version of the game by having bought  the Early Access copy of the game. If you wanted to come chat with us directly, it's a little easier in our Discord than the reviews! https://discord.gg/A6ebFaXmYN

reviews.review_description: Super.

As a kid, I spent hours playing SimCity, blissfully dragging roads and zoning neighborhoods from a god’s-eye view. Surviving Mars: Pioneer VR is like that… if the city constantly tried to kill you.

This isn’t about making the perfect utopia—it’s about clawing together a barely functioning dome while Mars hurls every disaster it can at you. And now, in VR, it’s personal. You don’t just place buildings—you stand inside them. You don’t just watch resources dwindle—you feel it.

It’s immersive, brutal, and addictively satisfying. Every victory feels earned. Every failure? Painfully your fault. Just like SimCity, only with more dust storms and existential dread.

Highly recommended for those who like their strategy with a side of panic.

(Product received for free to review)
Star Date: May 8th, 2025.
I am living in my spacious Martian home thinking how much you will enjoy this game if you like chill survival experiences. 

How did I get here? I mined a lot of resources like iron, copper, ice, etc. I fought through storms and my own need for food to build the power and structures needed to make this planet my own. The planet is fun to explore and feels just empty enough to make Mars feel right, and not just that this is early access. 

That said: This is early access and the development team is very active in both bug fixes, feature additions and improvements, and even taking reviewer feedback. I think I restarted the game 4x in my testing and review due to new builds changing things and realistically I was happy to do it. I enjoy the gameplay loop of building up my Mars base that much.

Graphics: 7/10 - For a quest title they are of course simple, but the Mars surface and the night sky look great.
Sound: 8/10 - Ice crackling, wind blowing, sounds muffling when you are in suit vs in your habitat. It feels just right
Gameplay: 8/10 - Good balance of watching your nutrition, oxygen, power consumption but not making it feel like you can't take time to just do whatever makes you happy. The resources respawn so you never have to feel like you are wasting things or need to make the "right choice" or get stuck
VR Mechanics: 7/10 good use of VR mechanics without going overboard and making you flail around just because it is in VR. From scanning, jetpacking, to driving the VR experience feels natural.

If you enjoy survival games pick this one up.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for helping test out SMP! You're input and bug reports really helped us make the game better.

I hope you revisit Mars after we make further adjustments, thanks for your kind review!

reviews.review_title: I got five kids to feed
reviews.review_description:
I've been playing this for a little while and seen it change so much over a short period of time, the Devs have really been killing it with the constant improvements and updates, so I'm going to not really go over any real issues it has as my review will be out of date within a week.
I've not really played many survival games, but this one is definitely on the easier side and is more of a gentle introduction to the genre, there are some enemies but the biggest issues you'll have is running out of oxygen, food isn't too hard to come by as you can live off of tea.
There's so much to do here, farming materials and sending off rocket caches to earn money, upgrading your suit, your booster, your farming weapon, driving a bike or a rover, discovering secrets and solving puzzles, it's already had a lot of content added over the past month.
For £15.99 it's a no brainer and a must buy for me.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words pioneer, and thanks for helping test. We'll continue to improve on the game to make the experience better and better. Excited to hear your thoughts on what we can add. https://discord.gg/a6Kq86eTFN

reviews.review_description:
I have almost finished the game and felt I needed to review it now, this is the first sandbox survival game I’ve played in vr that was any good, it has a decent base building system and decent survival mechanics, it’s very easy and allows you to focus more on growing your base and exploration which I personally enjoy, however if some areas could be a little more dangerous that wouldn’t be a bad addition.
Many have critiqued it for the very point and click nature of storage and crafting however I didn’t find this to be too immersion breaking. 
In terms of exploration it’s fairly stale, there’s enough to encourage you to go into a new area to find new materials, at least early on, late game there isn’t enough to craft yet in my opinion with the late game resources.Many of the structures you could explore were rather boring in my option, the crashed space ship near the start of the game was actualy very good but besides that the other dungeon like areas where you entered buildings to explore wernt very interesting and I found myself just rushing through, particularly as a lot of the loot wasn’t very interesting; one potential fix to this might be to add schematics for practical things in here rather than just scanning furniture.
I love base building in all games and in vr it’s even better, I’m glad I finally found a game that allows me to build a cozy house to my liking, it’s still early access however so this is limited, I would love to be able to select walls and turn them into windows much like subnautica; this is my biggest wish for the game, and also maybe a device or console you could place down to change the color of the base, perhaps with. Primary and secondary color for the body and the trim, which by default is white and blue. . One small nitpick, I would like to be able to change the words displayed above doors, most commonly these say “hallway”, perhaps I haven’t figured out how to yet but as far as I can tell you can’t do that, it’s a small detail but I’d like to be able to name these separate rooms e.g garden, crafting area etc. 

Summary of the features I’d like to see, generally just more environment and building related things
-windows (extremely important addition)
-ability to change the base color
-perhaps more visual affects/ graphics overall to make the world look more stunning, particularly during storms, especially if the above windows are added
-rework or revamp of almost all interior dungeon like areas; main solotuion would be to make them more hazardous, e.g patrolling bots, exposed wires etc

Overall this game is very enjoyable particularly for an early access game, it is missing a few details but the core gameplay is certainly there. Overall I’d strongly recommend this game to anyone who enjoys base building and more casual sandbox survival game enjoyers, those who prefer more combat orientated survival games probably won’t find much here though. So far I’d say it’s a 7/10, and can certainly see this game improving on when more personalisation and immersion features are added. Once I beat the game I will edit the review here at the bottom.
Loving the game. One of the games I can spend the longest in vr. So captivating. Awesome developers who seem very engaged.

developer_response.body: Thank you for giving SMP a try! We're glad you enjoy it.

reviews.review_description: WORST GAME I EVER PLAYED DO NOT BUY

developer_response.body: Hey Knight! We'd love to understand what made you dislike the game enough to leave this review! Please come chat with us on Discord (https://discord.gg/A6ebFaXmYN) or update this review with the issues you were encountering. Looking forward to speaking with you!

reviews.review_description: Really enjoyed playing the game. Kept my interest and played several days a week. Sadly I've completed the missions and there's really nothing interesting left to do. They should wipe the game and create more missions and maps to explore. I would be glad to get back to playing.

developer_response.body: We'll have some more stuff to do when the game leaves early access this year!

reviews.review_description: I love this game, are you kidding me it one of the best on the quest 3 to date. The graphics are fire!and the game play is great. The environments r great also. Plus you get theses vehicles for the planet also they are fun to drive around.especially the bike! Great work devs 👍

This is the first review I’ve ever written!
The game is absolutely worth it!! it’s a real pleasure to play. I caught myself a few times just staring at the Martian sky, admiring how beautiful the place is! 😀

Personally, I wish the structures looked a bit more realistic, but the mechanics and missions are excellent.

Having a great time on our red planet. It has been a perfect game to turn on my favorite music in the background and dive in to exploring and building. I like that it has been low stress for a lot of my experience so far, until it isn't. It gets really intense when you realize you are about to starve or run out of oxygen, or when a storm lowers visibility so much you have to navigate the terrain completely by using your map. The dev team is updating this very frequently and doing great at responding to feedback. This is a must have for your library.

I received a copy for playtesting but I treat reviews as if I had paid full price.  This is one of the few tests I've done where I'd be more than happy paying full price and it's not even a full release yet.

I've played a lot of the Survival Crafting genre and this is a solid addition.  You're watching your health, nutrition, and oxygen levels while also juggling inventory.  There are interesting places to explore and nice puzzles to solve.  Even though this has been easily the largest number of bugs I've ever reported in a test, the team has been on it and not only do they fix the bugs I'm finding but they make really clever decisions on how to fix them (such as automating the Jammer ramp so players don't get stuck inside it and adjusting the grid so assets line up well).  I honestly can't think of anything on the negative side to say about this because even game-breaking bugs can be easily avoided by being careful about saving your game (and they give you 5 save slots).  I feel like the difficulty level is generally good and it's not frustratingly difficult to explore and solve puzzles while surviving.

I highly recommend the game to anyone vaguely interested in a survival game set on Mars.  You won't be disappointed.  I've probably put more hours into this than most games I own and I won't be surprised if I double or triple it by the time they're fully released.  It's a very enjoyable game.

If you like Song in the smoke, Green Hell, Medieval Dynasty, ITR and other crafting and survival games then you are going to like this. It’s a great game with loads of potential and many hours of gameplay. I’ve amended my review because many of the small glitches have been resolved . Bear in mind that it is still in the early access stage with some occasional glitches like falling through buildings when entering or leaving them but generally it plays well. The menus system felt a little cumbersome at first but I’ve adapted to it.  It is seriously addictive . Good job game developers. I would like to see more options for furniture and development of the habitat. Also side missions that allow play after the mission is completed like in ITR would be good. I definitely recommend buying this game.

I think the beat the game because there aren't any more missions so now there's just nothing to do. And the game has started taking items out of my inventory and randomly putting them in the sky where I can't reach them. Including one of the crucial items the workbench so now I cant make anything new. I also researched items but then was never able to make those items.

developer_response.body: We are aware of this issue, and are working towards fixing this for the full release

reviews.review_title: I Love and Hate Surviving Mars
reviews.review_description:
FULL REVIEW AVAILABLE ON GAMERTAG VR YOUTUBE CHANNEL

For the last 4 days I’ve been playing Surving Mars Pioneer for about 2-3 hours at a time and so far I’ve only scratched the surface of what seems to be an absolutey huge game and most of the time I absolutely hate it. The inventory system is awful, it’s all check mark boxes, fast moving dialogue boxes and floating menus whiich makes it feel old and cumbersome, and if you play any other games and then go back to Surviving Mars Pioneer you have to spend a couple of minutes remembering how awful the inventory mechanism system is. 

Surving Mars also does that weird slowing down your walking speed if you turn which really pisses me off and It’s also insanely Grindy to the point it’s infuriating. The graphics range from really nice, fast and smooth to also dull and bland but after saying all of this, for some reason I can’t actually get enough of it.  

When I’m not playing Surving Mars Pioneer I’m thinking about it. While eating dinner, while walking the dog and even at the gym. I keep thinking about what elements I’ll find once I my scan of the new area of the map is complete

It has that Subnautica and No Mans Sky addiction. You put on the headset to have play a little bit more, then suddenly you’ve progressed or unlocked another mission or other mystery and before you know it 2 hours has gone by at the blink of an eye

It’s a game to just chill with and seemingly play for hours and when you see your first rocket take off, you feel a huge sense of achievement. It’s not perfect, it’s a little broken but it is in early access, it’s grindy but it has mystery, and a game loop design that keeps you coming back.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your thoughtful review Gamertag! We can't stop thinking about it :D
We'll continue to polish and improve the experience during the Early Access period. We hope that you check in after several months and let us know what you think of the updates.

reviews.review_title: Played all the way thru TWICE
reviews.review_description:
I love this game. Played through twice now and can’t wait for more. Controls (backpack/bldg) and constructing (make sure additions are Connected/GREEN :D) took a bit to get used to, not super intuitive- but definitely worth it. If you like base building/exploring/resource management this is for you. (Maybe like medieval dynasty on Mars?) The environment is a bit creepy, as it should be, at first but then turns relaxing once you’re familiar. 

The jet pack and bike mechanics are awesome and grinding for upgrades is addictive and satisfying. I’ve spent a ton of hours in it and hope there’s even more coming soon- would love to see a roadmap. Great game Devs!!

developer_response.body: We've been working on nailing down the roadmap! There will be a huge update pretty soon (originally we were going to release it last week but we decided to add something else into it :X). Look forward to hearing your thoughts on your 3rd playthrough!!

reviews.review_description:
Played the first couple of hours of this and thoroughly enjoyed it. It nails the Martian landscape and the feeling of being on an alien World. VR controls are intuitive, mining feels weighty and responsive. 
Pace is good for people like me who just like to take their time and explore rather than constantly fighting something. Really looking forward to see how this one develops through Early Access. 

Only thing I really want is the ability to turn off the helmet when outside.

Haven’t played to long but I want to say it’s a beautiful game great concept and I love crafting and survival games. So much fun and I hope they keep making content. When I play longer ill add to my review but it’s worth buying. Thanks
